Loader module

Loader.get_dataset_config(dataset_name: str) → dict[source]

Retrieve configuration parameters for a dataset.

Parameters:

dataset_name (str) – Name of the dataset (“CIFAR10” or “MNIST”).

Returns:

Dictionary containing dataset parameters:

  • ’n_channel’: number of channels in the images

  • ’img_size’: image height and width

  • ’transform’: torchvision transform applied to the dataset

  • ’loader’: torchvision dataset class

Return type:

dict

Raises:

ValueError – If dataset_name is not recognized.

Loader.get_loader(directory='./dataset', batch_size=128, train=True, dataset_name='CIFAR10', num_workers=0, pin_memory=True)[source]

Create a PyTorch DataLoader for a given dataset.

Parameters:

  • directory (str) – Root path where datasets are stored. The dataset loader will create a subfolder with the dataset name automatically.

  • batch_size (int) – Number of images per batch.

  • train (bool) – Whether to load the training split (True) or test split (False).

  • dataset_name (str) – Name of the dataset to load (“CIFAR10” or “MNIST”).

  • num_workers (int) – Number of subprocesses to use for data loading. Defaults to 0 (useful for Windows).

  • pin_memory (bool) – If True, the data loader will copy tensors into CUDA pinned memory before returning them. Useful when training on GPU.

Returns:

PyTorch DataLoader for the specified dataset.

Return type:

DataLoader

Raises:

ValueError – If dataset_name is not supported.
